metadata.note: Jihadist armed groups across the Sahel + Lake Chad basin. Terrain-gated (AFRICA-SAHEL, AFRICA-NIGERIA) plus the groups' own NON-STATE centroids -- 'Boko Haram'/'Islamic State'/'al Qaeda' audited at 100% %foreign against those three centroids, which is a CENTROID GAP (spec §4), not a leak: they are this FN's own actors. primary_target=null (multi-country phenomenon; any target deletes three quarters of it). Tuareg separatism is deliberately NOT here -- see sahel_tuareg_separatism. DROPPED after pre-audit against the real matcher: Ansaroul/Macina/Katiba/garrison/Tinzaouaten (0 hits), ambush (2, Nigeria banditry), barracks (1, a child-abuse story), extremist (3), terrorist (86 but 49% foreign and generic news vocabulary -- spec Pillar 3 ✗), coup (belongs to junta atomic), checkpoint (2 hits, one a Lagos police shooting). ADDED after the first narrative pass measured sahel_counterinsurgency_abuses at 1 title: the counterinsurgency-harm theme is large and real (HRW on army exactions since April, 'Burkina Faso: Army behind most civilian deaths', the Nigerian Air Force strike on a Borno market, Africa Corps 'atrocités') but most of that coverage never names an armed group, so the group-name bundle could not see it. airstrike/air strike/civilian are Pillar-4 domain actions and target-nouns, not stance vocabulary. DROPPED from that addition after audit: abduct (55) and kidnap (44) -- Nigerian banditry and ordinary crime ('kidnappers targeting billionaires in Lagos'), troops (54, 69% foreign -- the US-deployment-to-Nigeria story, a different phenomenon), soldiers (29, generic).

fr: Groupe de soutien à l'islam et aux musulmans

bundle.term: Counterinsurgency operations and their civilian toll
